app.tagline: Place eight queens so no two share a row, a column, or a diagonal. The table keeps score.
app.title: Eight Queens

edu.backtracking_body: The computer places queens one column at a time. If a position is safe, it tries the next column. If it gets stuck, it *backtracks* — removes the last queen and tries a different row. This continues until all 8 queens are placed safely.
edu.fact_12: If you count rotations and reflections as the same, there are only **12 truly different** solutions
edu.fact_1848: The puzzle was first published in the *Berliner Schachzeitung* in **1848**
edu.fact_92: There are exactly **92 solutions** to the Eight Queens puzzle
edu.fact_hard: For an n×n board with n queens, finding solutions gets *very* hard as n grows. It's used to teach algorithms in computer science
edu.intro: The challenge: place **8 queens** on a chessboard so that no queen can attack any other. A queen attacks every square on her row, column, and both diagonals.
edu.title: What is the Eight Queens puzzle?

scoresheet.dot_caption: Every dot is one valid arrangement.
scoresheet.heading: Scoresheet

status.board_full: Board is full — remove a queen first
status.start: Place 8 queens so none can attack each other
status.under_attack: Can't place here — this cell is under attack
